title: Domestication of L0 Parts for Loop Type IIS (BsaI and SapI)
author: Eftychis Frangedakis
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.92jh8cn
materials: Amplify the parts using your preferred polymerase (e.g. Phusion, Thermo Scientigic™) following the manufacturer instructions.
Extract the amplified fragments using a PCR purification kit (e.g. Qiagen MinElute™ ).

steps.title: Both primer 2 and 3 will...
steps.instruction: Both primer 2 and 3 will be specially designed with a single nucleotide mismatch to alter the sequence to domesticate (taking care to not alter amino acid composition if the region is in the coding sequence). Primers will also contain SapI recognition sites to allow the two fragments to be ligated together into pUAP4 using SapI type IIS assembly. Primer 1 and primer 4 are designed according to the guidelines for cloning of L0 parts into pUAP4 . Blue arrows: SapI recognition site. Blue dashed lines: SapI cleavage site. Red arrows: BsaI recognition site. CmR: chloramphenicol bacterial resistance cassette. LacZ: lacZα cassette for blue-white screening of colonies.

steps.title: Run your PCR product on agarose gel
steps.instruction: Run your PCR product on agarose gel.

steps.title: Set up the assembly of the...
steps.instruction: Set up the assembly of the fragments as in cloning of L0 parts into pUAP4 [pUAP4] = plasmid length/200 [fragment] = fragment length/100 The formulas above will give you the required concentration in ng/µl.
